root_text.an9.34:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā sāriputto rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.an9.34:1.2: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an9.34:1.5: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā udāyī āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an9.34:1.6: “kiṁ panettha, āvuso sāriputta, sukhaṁ yadettha natthi vedayitan”ti?
root_text.an9.34:1.7: “Etadeva khvettha, āvuso, sukhaṁ yadettha natthi vedayitaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:1.10: Cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā,
root_text.an9.34:1.14: kāyaviññeyyā phoṭṭhabbā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā—
root_text.an9.34:1.16: Yaṁ kho, āvuso, ime pañca kāmaguṇe paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, idaṁ vuccatāvuso, kāmasukhaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:2.1: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:2.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato kāmasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:2.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:2.4: evamevassa te kāmasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:2.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:2.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā …pe… dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:3.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato vitakkasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:3.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:3.4: evamevassa te vitakkasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:3.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:3.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu pītiyā ca virāgā …pe… tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:4.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato pītisahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:4.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:4.4: evamevassa te pītisahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:4.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:4.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sukhassa ca pahānā …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:5.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato upekkhāsahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:5.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:5.4: evamevassa te upekkhāsahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:5.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:5.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ananto ākāsoti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:6.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato rūpasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:6.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:6.4: evamevassa te rūpasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:6.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:6.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma anantaṁ viññāṇanti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:7.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato ākāsānañcāyatanasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:7.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:7.4: evamevassa te ākāsānañcāyatanasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:7.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:7.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma, natthi kiñcīti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:8.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato viññāṇañcāyatanasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:8.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:8.4: evamevassa te viññāṇañcāyatanasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:8.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:8.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.34:9.2: Tassa ce, āvuso, bhikkhuno iminā vihārena viharato ākiñcaññāyatanasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti, svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:9.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sukhino dukkhaṁ uppajjeyya yāvadeva ābādhāya;
root_text.an9.34:9.4: evamevassa te ākiñcaññāyatanasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti. Svassa hoti ābādho.
root_text.an9.34:9.5: Yo kho panāvuso, ābādho dukkhametaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā.
root_text.an9.34:9.6: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānaṁ.
root_text.an9.34:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati, paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti.
root_text.an9.34:11.1: Imināpi kho etaṁ, āvuso, pariyāyena veditabbaṁ yathā sukhaṁ nibbānan”ti.

translation_text.an9.34:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.an9.34:1.5: When he said this, Venerable Udāyī said to him,
translation_text.an9.34:1.6: “But Reverend Sāriputta, what’s blissful about it, since nothing is felt?”
translation_text.an9.34:1.7: “The fact that nothing is felt is precisely what’s blissful about it.
translation_text.an9.34:1.8: Reverend, there are these five kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.an9.34:1.10: Sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.an9.34:1.14: Touches known by the body, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.an9.34:1.15: These are the five kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.an9.34:1.16: The pleasure and happiness that arise from these five kinds of sensual stimulation is called sensual pleasure.
translation_text.an9.34:2.1: First, take a mendicant who, quite secluded from sensual pleasures … enters and remains in the first absorption.
translation_text.an9.34:2.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by sensual pleasures beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:2.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:2.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by sensual pleasures beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:2.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:2.6: That’s the way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:3.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, enters and remains in the second absorption.
translation_text.an9.34:3.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by placing of the mind beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:3.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:3.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by placing of the mind beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:3.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:3.6: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:4.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, with the fading away of rapture, enters and remains in the third absorption.
translation_text.an9.34:4.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by rapture beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:4.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:4.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by rapture beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:4.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:4.6: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:5.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, with the giving up of pleasure and pain and the disappearance of former happiness and sadness, enters and remains in the fourth absorption.
translation_text.an9.34:5.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by bliss with equanimity beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:5.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:5.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by bliss with equanimity beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:5.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:5.6: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:6.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that ‘space is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an9.34:6.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by form beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:6.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:6.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by form beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:6.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:6.6: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:7.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite space, aware that ‘consciousness is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite consciousness.
translation_text.an9.34:7.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by the dimension of infinite space beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:7.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:7.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by the dimension of infinite space beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:7.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:7.6: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:8.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, enters and remains in the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.an9.34:8.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by the dimension of infinite consciousness beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:8.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:8.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by the dimension of infinite consciousness beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:8.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:8.6: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:9.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond the dimension of nothingness, enters and remains in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an9.34:9.2: While a mendicant is practicing such a meditation, if perception and focus accompanied by the dimension of nothingness beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:9.3: Suppose a happy person were to experience pain; that would be an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:9.4: In the same way, should perception and focus accompanied by the dimension of nothingness beset them, that’s an affliction for them.
translation_text.an9.34:9.5: And affliction has been called suffering by the Buddha.
translation_text.an9.34:9.6: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.
translation_text.an9.34:10.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, enters and remains in the cessation of perception and feeling. And, having seen with wisdom, their defilements come to an end.
translation_text.an9.34:11.1: That too is a way to understand how extinguishment is bliss.”
